summary: Close M2-R10 through M2-R12 with dependency-aware empty-viewer guidance, localized and grouped keybinding settings, and a focusable Help explanation, each proved by assertion-level RED and focused runtime tests.
specSections: Section 2.7 test-first evidence and milestone gates

decisions.decision: Propagate ready, checking, and unavailable engine state into the empty model viewer and display the next action that is actually available in each state.
decisions.rationale: An unavailable render control cannot honestly be the empty pane's requested action. State-specific catalog copy satisfies N-7 without hiding editing or engine configuration paths.

decisions.decision: Render every Appendix D command with a catalog-backed human label inside Files, Editor, Render, Viewer, or Layout fieldsets while retaining stable command identifiers only in application state.
decisions.rationale: Raw camelCase implementation identifiers are not user copy and cannot be translated. Explicit groups improve scanability while leaving serialization and command routing unchanged.

decisions.decision: Replace the disabled title-only Help button with an enabled, focusable disclosure whose visible role=status explanation is named for assistive technology.
decisions.rationale: Disabled controls are not normally keyboard-focusable, so their title cannot be the sole explanation. A small disclosure preserves honest scope while making the explanation discoverable.

testEvidence.test: Focused viewer, settings, and menu tests assert unavailable-engine next-step copy, engine-state propagation, localized grouped labels with no raw command identifiers, and a focusable Help disclosure with an accessible status name.

red.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/ui/viewer/ViewerPane.test.tsx tests/ui/settings/SettingsDialog.test.tsx tests/ui/layout/WebMenuBar.test.tsx --reporter=verbose; pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/ui/viewer/ViewerPaneConnector.test.tsx -t "forwards engine availability" --reporter=verbose
red.outcome: ASSERTION-LEVEL RED: Help was disabled; the unavailable-engine next-step message was absent after the lazy viewer resolved; human keybinding labels were absent in both normal and collision flows; and ViewerPaneConnector forwarded undefined instead of false.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/ui/viewer/ViewerPane.test.tsx tests/ui/viewer/ViewerPaneConnector.test.tsx tests/ui/viewer/ModelViewer.theme.test.tsx tests/ui/settings/SettingsDialog.test.tsx tests/ui/layout/WebMenuBar.test.tsx --reporter=verbose
green.outcome: GREEN: Test Files 5 passed (5); Tests 48 passed (48); Duration 1.91s.

testEvidence.test: Typecheck all component contracts and lint the expanded message/settings/viewer/menu surface under the 400-line and platform-boundary policies.

red.command: Not applicable after assertion-level component RED; no static-only defect was used as TDD evidence.

green.outcome: GREEN: TypeScript exited 0. Biome checked 271 files with no fixes; source policy passed and every UI file remained at or below 400 lines.

questions: M2-R10, M2-R11, and M2-R12 are closed by this slice; no specification question blocks their behavior.
The first unavailable-viewer RED initially inspected the Suspense fallback. The test was strengthened to await the lazy viewer, then failed on the missing intended message before implementation.
No owner similarity gate, comparison repository, workflow content, or prohibited source was opened or executed.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
